words.definitionEn: done as part of a plan that is meant to achieve a particular purpose or to gain an advantage
words.exampleEn: strategic planning

words.definitionEn: interesting and unusual enough to attract attention
words.exampleEn: a striking feature

words.definitionEn: a long narrow piece of paper, metal, cloth, etc.
words.exampleEn: a strip of material

words.definitionEn: to take off all or most of your clothes or another person’s clothes
words.exampleEn: I stripped and washed myself all over.

words.definitionEn: to try very hard to achieve something
words.exampleEn: strive for something, We encourage all members to strive for the highest standards.

words.definitionEn: connected with the way in which something is built or organized
words.exampleEn: Storms have caused structural damage to hundreds of homes.

words.definitionEn: a long seat for two or more people, usually made of wood
words.exampleEn: a park bench

words.definitionEn: to hit your foot against something while you are walking or running and almost fall
words.exampleEn: The child stumbled and fell.

words.definitionEn: to make a person or an animal unconscious for a short time, especially by hitting them on the head
words.exampleEn: The fall stunned me for a moment.

words.definitionEn: something that can be measured and used as a standard that other things can be compared with
words.exampleEn: Tests at the age of seven provide a benchmark against which the child's progress at school can be measured.

words.definitionEn: the act of accepting that somebody has defeated you and that you must obey them
words.exampleEn: a gesture of submission

words.definitionEn: a person who pays money, usually once a year, to receive regular copies of a magazine or newspaper or have access to it online
words.exampleEn: subscribers to ‘New Scientist’

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that you pay regularly to receive a service, be a member of a club, support a charity or receive regular copies of a newspaper or magazine; the act of paying this money
words.exampleEn: an annual subscription

words.definitionEn: money that is paid by a government or an organization to reduce the costs of services or of producing goods so that their prices can be kept low
words.exampleEn: agricultural subsidies

words.definitionEn: large in amount, value or importance
words.exampleEn: substantial sums of money

words.definitionEn: very much; a lot
words.exampleEn: The costs have increased substantially.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that you use or have instead of the one you normally use or have
words.exampleEn: a meat substitute

words.definitionEn: to take the place of somebody/something else; to use somebody/something instead of somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: substitute for somebody/something, Nothing can substitute for the advice your doctor is able to give you.

words.definitionEn: an act of using one person or thing in the place of another
words.exampleEn: Two substitutions were made during the game.

words.definitionEn: not very obvious or easy to notice
words.exampleEn: subtle colours/flavours/smells, etc.

words.definitionEn: in or connected with a suburb
words.exampleEn: suburban areas

words.definitionEn: a number of people or things that follow each other in time or order
words.exampleEn: a succession of visitors

words.definitionEn: following immediately one after the other
words.exampleEn: This was their fourth successive win.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that comes after somebody/something else and takes their/its place
words.exampleEn: Who's the likely successor to him as party leader?

words.definitionEn: to take liquid, air, etc. into your mouth by using the muscles of your lips
words.exampleEn: to suck the juice from an orange

words.definitionEn: to make a claim against a person or an organization in court about something that they have said or done to harm you
words.exampleEn: They threatened to sue if the work was not completed.

words.definitionEn: in or to a lower position than somebody/something; under somebody/something
words.exampleEn: They found the body buried beneath a pile of leaves.

words.definitionEn: the act of killing yourself deliberately
words.exampleEn: to commit suicide

words.definitionEn: a set of rooms, especially in a hotel
words.exampleEn: a hotel/private/honeymoon suite

words.definitionEn: the highest point of something, especially the top of a mountain
words.exampleEn: We reached the summit at noon.

words.definitionEn: a person who gains as a result of something
words.exampleEn: Who will be the main beneficiary of the cuts in income tax?

words.definitionEn: excellent; of very good quality
words.exampleEn: a superb player

words.definitionEn: better in quality than somebody/something else; greater than somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: vastly superior

words.definitionEn: to be in charge of somebody/something and make sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.
words.exampleEn: supervise (somebody/something), to supervise building work

words.definitionEn: the work or activity involved in being in charge of somebody/something and making sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.
words.exampleEn: Very young children should not be left to play without supervision.

words.definitionEn: a person who supervises somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I have a meeting with my supervisor about my research topic.

words.definitionEn: a thing that is added to something else to improve or complete it
words.exampleEn: vitamin/dietary supplements (= vitamins and other foods eaten in addition to what you usually eat)

words.definitionEn: to add something to something in order to improve it or make it more complete
words.exampleEn: supplement something with something, a diet supplemented with vitamin pills

words.definitionEn: encouraging somebody or giving them help or sympathy
words.exampleEn: a supportive family

words.definitionEn: according to what is generally thought or believed but not known for certain
words.exampleEn: The novel is supposedly based on a true story.

words.definitionEn: to put an end, often by force, to a group or an activity that is believed to threaten authority
words.exampleEn: The rebellion was brutally suppressed.

words.definitionEn: highest in rank or position
words.exampleEn: the Supreme Commander of the armed forces

words.definitionEn: a sudden increase of a strong feeling
words.exampleEn: She felt a sudden surge of anger.

words.definitionEn: to move quickly and with force in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: The gates opened and the crowd surged forward.

words.definitionEn: used in or connected with surgery
words.exampleEn: surgical procedures

words.definitionEn: an amount that is extra or more than you need
words.exampleEn: food surpluses

words.definitionEn: to admit that you have been defeated and want to stop fighting; to allow yourself to be caught, taken prisoner, etc.
words.exampleEn: The rebel soldiers were forced to surrender.

words.definitionEn: the act of carefully watching a person suspected of a crime or a place where a crime may be committed
words.exampleEn: The police are keeping the suspects under constant surveillance.

words.definitionEn: a statement saying that you think a person is guilty of doing something wrong or illegal; the fact of accusing somebody
words.exampleEn: I don't want to make an accusation until I have some proof.

words.definitionEn: the act of officially removing somebody from their job, school, team, etc. for a period of time, usually as a punishment
words.exampleEn: suspension from school

words.definitionEn: a feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, even though you have no proof
words.exampleEn: They drove away slowly to avoid arousing suspicion.

words.definitionEn: feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, without having any proof
words.exampleEn: They became suspicious of his behaviour and contacted the police.

words.definitionEn: to provide enough of what somebody/something needs in order to live or exist
words.exampleEn: Which planets can sustain life?

words.definitionEn: a swinging movement or rhythm
words.exampleEn: He took a wild swing at the ball.

words.definitionEn: to move backwards or forwards or from side to side while hanging from a fixed point; to make something do this
words.exampleEn: His arms swung as he walked.

words.definitionEn: a weapon with a long metal blade (= sharp cutting part) and a handle
words.exampleEn: to draw/sheathe a sword (= to take it out of/put it into its cover)

words.definitionEn: containing symbols, or being used as a symbol
words.exampleEn: He shook his fist in a symbolic gesture of defiance.

words.definitionEn: a set of physical conditions that show you have a particular disease or medical problem
words.exampleEn: PMS or premenstrual syndrome

words.definitionEn: the act of combining separate ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.; a mixture or combination of ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.
words.exampleEn: synthesis of A with B, the synthesis of art with everyday life

words.definitionEn: done according to a system or plan, in a complete, efficient or determined way
words.exampleEn: a systematic approach to solving the problem

words.definitionEn: an act of trying to take the ball from an opponent in football (soccer), hockey, etc.; an act of knocking somebody to the ground, for example in rugby or American football
words.exampleEn: He was booked for a late tackle on Torres.

words.definitionEn: the particular method you use to achieve something
words.exampleEn: They tried all kinds of tactics to get us to go.

words.definitionEn: connected with the particular method you use to achieve something
words.exampleEn: tactical planning

words.definitionEn: a person who pays tax to the government, especially on the money that they earn
words.exampleEn: Hundreds of thousands of pounds of taxpayers' money (= money paid in taxes) have been spent on the project.

words.definitionEn: to give information about somebody/something to an enemy
words.exampleEn: betray somebody/something, He was offered money to betray his colleagues.

words.definitionEn: to attract somebody or make somebody want to do or have something, even if they know it is wrong
words.exampleEn: tempt somebody, I was tempted by the dessert menu.

words.definitionEn: a person who pays rent for the use of a room, building, land, etc. to the person who owns it
words.exampleEn: They had evicted their tenants for non-payment of rent.

words.definitionEn: kind, gentle and loving
words.exampleEn: tender words

words.definitionEn: the period of time when somebody holds an important job, especially a political one; the act of holding an important job
words.exampleEn: his four-year tenure as president

words.definitionEn: that cannot be cured and will lead to death, often slowly
words.exampleEn: He has terminal lung cancer.

words.definitionEn: to end; to make something end
words.exampleEn: Your contract of employment terminates in December.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to an area of land when you are mentioning its natural features, for example, if it is rough, flat, etc.
words.exampleEn: difficult/rough/mountainous terrain

words.definitionEn: excellent; wonderful
words.exampleEn: I feel absolutely terrific today!

words.definitionEn: to make a statement that something happened or that something is true, especially as a witness in court
words.exampleEn: testify against somebody/something, She refused to testify against her husband.

words.definitionEn: a thing that shows that something else exists or is true
words.exampleEn: This increase in exports bears testimony to the successes of industry.

words.definitionEn: the way a surface, substance or piece of cloth feels when you touch it, for example how rough, smooth, hard or soft it is
words.exampleEn: the soft texture of velvet

words.definitionEn: used to show that you are pleased that something good has happened or that something bad has been avoided
words.exampleEn: There was a fire in the building, but thankfully no one was hurt.

words.definitionEn: connected with the theatre
words.exampleEn: a theatrical agent

words.definitionEn: the study of religion and beliefs
words.exampleEn: a degree in theology

words.definitionEn: connected with the ideas and principles on which a particular subject is based, rather than with practice and experiment
words.exampleEn: a theoretical approach

words.definitionEn: after the time or event mentioned
words.exampleEn: She married at 17 and gave birth to her first child shortly thereafter.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the result of the action or situation mentioned
words.exampleEn: Regular exercise strengthens the heart, thereby reducing the risk of heart attack.

words.definitionEn: making people think seriously about a particular subject or issue
words.exampleEn: a brilliant and thought-provoking play

words.definitionEn: quiet, because you are thinking
words.exampleEn: He looked thoughtful.

words.definitionEn: a thin string of cotton, wool, silk, etc. used for sewing or making cloth
words.exampleEn: a needle and thread

words.definitionEn: the floor or ground at the bottom of a doorway, considered as the entrance to a building or room
words.exampleEn: He stepped across the threshold.

words.definitionEn: very excited and pleased
words.exampleEn: ‘Are you pleased?’ ‘I'm thrilled.’

words.definitionEn: to become, and continue to be, successful, strong, healthy, etc.
words.exampleEn: New businesses thrive in this area.

words.definitionEn: a regular rise and fall in the level of the sea, caused by the pull of the moon and sun; the flow of water that happens as the sea rises and falls
words.exampleEn: the ebb and flow of the tide

words.definitionEn: to hold or fix something more securely in position; to make something or become more difficult to move, open or separate
words.exampleEn: tighten something, to tighten a lid/screw/rope/knot

words.definitionEn: trees that are grown to be used in building or for making things
words.exampleEn: the timber industry

words.definitionEn: a person who is on trial for committing a crime
words.exampleEn: The accused was found innocent.

words.definitionEn: happening at exactly the right time
words.exampleEn: A nasty incident was prevented by the timely arrival of the police.

words.definitionEn: the dried leaves of the tobacco plant that are used for making cigarettes, smoking in a pipe, etc.
words.exampleEn: tobacco smoke

words.definitionEn: the quality of being willing to accept or tolerate somebody/something, especially opinions or behaviour that you may not agree with, or people who are not like you
words.exampleEn: She had no tolerance for jokes of any kind.

words.definitionEn: to allow somebody to do something that you do not agree with or like
words.exampleEn: tolerate something, Their relationship was tolerated but not encouraged.

words.definitionEn: money that you pay to use a particular road or bridge
words.exampleEn: motorway tolls

words.definitionEn: to be higher than a particular amount
words.exampleEn: Worldwide sales look set to top $1 billion.

words.definitionEn: the act of causing somebody severe pain in order to punish them or make them say or do something
words.exampleEn: Many of the refugees have suffered torture.

words.definitionEn: to hurt somebody physically or mentally in order to punish them or make them tell you something
words.exampleEn: torture somebody, Many of the rebels were captured and tortured by secret police.

words.definitionEn: to throw something lightly or carelessly
words.exampleEn: toss something + adv./prep., I tossed the book aside and got up.

words.definitionEn: to reach a particular total
words.exampleEn: Imports totalled $1.5 billion last year.

words.definitionEn: containing poison; poisonous
words.exampleEn: toxic chemicals/fumes/gases/substances

words.definitionEn: a mark, an object or a sign that shows that somebody/something existed or was present
words.exampleEn: It's exciting to discover traces of earlier civilizations.

words.definitionEn: to tie somebody/something with rope, string, etc. so that they/it cannot move or are held together strongly
words.exampleEn: bind somebody/something to something, She was bound to a chair.

words.definitionEn: a name, symbol or design that a company uses for its products and that cannot be used by anyone else
words.exampleEn: ‘Big Mac’ is McDonald's best-known trademark.

words.definitionEn: the story of a person’s life written by somebody else; this type of writing
words.exampleEn: Boswell’s biography of Johnson

words.definitionEn: a long line or series of marks that is left by somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a trail of blood

words.definitionEn: to pull something behind somebody/something, usually along the ground; to be pulled along in this way
words.exampleEn: trail something, A jeep trailing a cloud of dust was speeding in my direction.

words.definitionEn: a truck, or a container with wheels, that is pulled by another vehicle
words.exampleEn: a car towing a trailer with a boat on it

words.definitionEn: a piece of business that is done between people, especially an act of buying or selling
words.exampleEn: financial transactions between companies

words.definitionEn: a written or printed copy of words that have been spoken
words.exampleEn: a transcript of the interview

words.definitionEn: a complete change in somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The way in which we work has undergone a complete transformation in the past decade.

words.definitionEn: the process of being moved or carried from one place to another
words.exampleEn: The cost includes transit.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of passing something from one person, place or thing to another
words.exampleEn: the transmission of the disease

words.definitionEn: the quality of something, such as a situation or an argument, that makes it easy to understand
words.exampleEn: a need for greater transparency in legal documents

words.definitionEn: allowing you to see through it
words.exampleEn: The insect's wings are almost transparent.

words.definitionEn: a mental condition caused by severe shock, stress or fear, especially when the harmful effects last for a long time
words.exampleEn: the effects of trauma and stress on the body

words.definitionEn: a formal agreement between two or more countries
words.exampleEn: the Treaty of Rome

words.definitionEn: very great
words.exampleEn: a tremendous explosion

words.definitionEn: connected with a tribe or tribes
words.exampleEn: tribal art

words.definitionEn: a type of court with the authority to deal with a particular problem or disagreement
words.exampleEn: an international war crimes tribunal

words.definitionEn: an act, a statement or a gift that is intended to show your love or respect, especially for a dead person
words.exampleEn: At her funeral her oldest friend paid tribute to her life and work.

words.definitionEn: the part of a gun that you press in order to fire it
words.exampleEn: to pull/squeeze the trigger

words.definitionEn: a group of three people or things
words.exampleEn: A trio of English runners featured in the women’s 1 500 metres.

words.definitionEn: a great success, achievement or victory
words.exampleEn: one of the greatest triumphs of modern science

words.definitionEn: an object such as a silver cup that is given as a prize for winning a competition
words.exampleEn: a trophy cabinet

words.definitionEn: worried and anxious
words.exampleEn: She looked into his troubled face.

words.definitionEn: a person or an organization that has control of money or property that has been put into a trust for somebody
words.exampleEn: The bank will act as trustees for the estate until the child is 18.

words.definitionEn: a senior priest in charge of the work of the Church in a city or district
words.exampleEn: the Bishop of Oxford

words.definitionEn: the act of teaching something, especially to one person or to people in small groups
words.exampleEn: She received private tuition in French.

words.definitionEn: the number of people who attend a particular event
words.exampleEn: This year's festival attracted a record turnout.

words.definitionEn: the total amount of goods or services sold by a company during a particular period of time
words.exampleEn: an annual turnover of $75 million

words.definitionEn: the action of turning something with your hand, or of turning a part of your body
words.exampleEn: She gave the lid another twist and it came off.

words.definitionEn: to bend or turn something into a particular shape
words.exampleEn: Twist the wire to form a circle.
